record.text:
Pick the camp before the tool:

– Camp 1: predictable, locked in
– Camp 2: swap models, cap spend
– Camp 3: flat bill, OSS only

All 7 compared:
www.deployhq.com/blog/claude-...

The agents + IDEs that plug into the routers:

• Aider — terminal, git-native
• Cline — autonomous IDE agent, MCP-native
• Continue.dev — Apache 2.0, VS Code + JetBrains
• Cursor — single-vendor, hybrid pricing

Mix-and-match is the point.

3/ Flat-rate + curated OSS.

OpenCode: MIT-licensed, 160k+ GitHub stars, 900+ contributors.

Optional flat-rate Go subscription for curated Chinese OSS models. One bill, no metering. Ceiling below frontier, but solid for daily coding.
